articles.title: Locked In or Left Open: A Decision Matrix for PTAB Fact Findings After Appeal
articles.content:
丹尼斯·克劳奇撰

PTO上诉委员会（PTAB）原始决定中的附带事实认定，在联邦巡回法院撤销并发回重审后，将何去何从？核心问题是：PTAB在发回重审时，能否修改未在上诉中涉及的问题？在Intellectual Pixels Ltd. v. Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC案中，No. 24-2174（联邦巡回法院，2026年7月10日），法院提供了一个决定矩阵，尝试在此重现。

该矩阵将新判决与法院对比Bitmanagement Software GmbH v. United States, 124 F.4th 1368（联邦巡回法院，2025年）的处理方式相结合。行代表委员会声明是否为上诉判决的依据（还是附带意见）；列代表上诉判决对该问题的处理：

|上诉中已解决（明确或必要推论）|上诉中未涉及|
|声明为判决依据|属于指令。委员会必须在发回重审时遵从，无论法院如何裁决。|锁定。该声明属于上诉判决范围内且未被触及；依据Bitmanagement，此类认定"在此阶段不能被质疑。"|
|声明对判决非必要|在上诉中实际解决的范围内具有约束力；必要推论可涉及无人辩论的问题。|发回重审时开放。委员会可自由以任一方式裁决该问题。|

这是索尼针对美国专利第10,681,109号提出的多方复审（IPR2021-00237）挑战第二次诉至联邦巡回法院。该专利涉及交互式应用的服务器端托管：服务器接收用户输入，生成更新图像，压缩并传输至瘦客户端显示。主要对比文献美国专利第6,409,602号（Wiltshire）披露了一种服务器托管游戏系统，其中服务器传输"压缩视频MPEG流"，其对"游戏"的定义明确包括《毁灭战士》。

在第一份最终书面决定中，委员会支持专利权人IPL，将Wiltshire解释为仅披露服务器端选择预存图像而非生成新图像。联邦巡回法院在非先例意见中撤销了该决定：Wiltshire的图2操作适用于《毁灭战士》类游戏，且各方认可《毁灭战士》需要生成新图像。Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC v. Intellectual Pixels Ltd., No. 22-2118, 2023 WL 6773879（联邦巡回法院，2023年10月13日）。发回重审后，委员会推翻原决定，认定权利要求不可专利。IPL在本次上诉中的主要论点是，委员会的出尔反尔违反了指令。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

What happens to stray fact findings in an original PTAB decision after the Federal Circuit vacates and remands? The basic question: can the PTAB alter its decision on remand regarding issues that were not addressed in the appeal? In Intellectual Pixels Ltd. v. Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC, No. 24-2174 (Fed. Cir. July 10, 2026), the court offered something of a decision matrix that I have attempted to recreate here.

The matrix combines the new decision with the court's treatment of Bitmanagement Software GmbH v. United States, 124 F.4th 1368 (Fed. Cir. 2025). The rows ask whether the Board's statement was a basis for the judgment that went up on appeal (or instead dicta); the columns ask what the appellate decision did with the issue:

Issue resolved in original appeal (explicitly or by necessary implication) Issue not addressed in original appeal
Statement was a basis for the appealed judgment Part of the mandate. The Board must conform on remand, whichever way the court resolved it. Locked in. The statement sits within the scope of the appealed judgment and was left undisturbed; per Bitmanagement, such findings "cannot be challenged at this stage."
Statement was unnecessary to the judgment Binding to the extent actually resolved on appeal; necessary implication can reach points nobody briefed. Open on remand. The Board remains free to decide the issue either way.

This is the second trip to the Federal Circuit for Sony's inter partes review challenge (IPR2021-00237) to U.S. Patent No. 10,681,109, which claims server-side hosting of an interactive application: the server receives user inputs, generates updated images, and compresses and transmits them to a thin client for display. The primary reference, U.S. Patent No. 6,409,602 (Wiltshire), disclosed a server-hosted gaming system in which the server transmits a "compressed video MPEG stream," and its definition of "game" expressly included Doom.

In the first final written decision, the Board sided with patent owner IPL, reading Wiltshire to disclose only server-side selection of pre-stored images rather than generation of new ones. The Federal Circuit vacated in a nonprecedential opinion: Wiltshire's Figure 2 operation applies to games like Doom, and the parties agreed that Doom requires generating new images. Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC v. Intellectual Pixels Ltd., No. 22-2118, 2023 WL 6773879 (Fed. Cir. Oct. 13, 2023). On remand, the Board flipped its decision and found the claims unpatentable. IPL's lead argument in this second appeal was that the Board's about-face violated the mandate.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.

articles.title: Looking Forward: Why Completed Conduct Often Cannot Support a Preliminary Injunction
articles.content:
联邦巡回法院已在同一宗卷帘门专利纠纷中两次驳回了临时禁令救济请求。在Ridge Corp.诉Kirk NationaLease Co.一案（联邦巡回法院，2026年7月13日，第25-1254号）中，Mayer法官代表合议庭一致裁定，撤销了禁止被告制造和销售其竞争性单面板保温卡车门的禁令。法院认定存在三个独立权利要求限制的不侵权重大疑问，并将不可弥补损害的证明称为“最多只是推测性的”。

临时禁令是面向未来的救济，而支持该禁令的两种理论（本案的两项不侵权主张：虚假专利标记和州法侵权干扰）完全建立在已完成的过去行为之上：一则将被指控的门描述为“已获专利”的广告，而该专利申请当时仍在审批中；以及Kirk专利律师致Whiting Door Manufacturing的一封信，警告其可能因与Ridge合作生产单面板门而面临专利使用费责任。由于原告未能提供任何证据证明上述任一行为可能重现，因此均无法支持针对未来行为的禁令。

合议庭参考了最近的宪法第一修正案案件Murthy诉密苏里州案（603 U.S. 43 (2024)）以寻求支持。该案即社交媒体“施压”案，涉及拜登政府推动社交媒体公司删除或降级关于新冠疫情和选举篡改的虚假信息。最高法院未就政府行为是否违宪作出裁决，而是6比3裁定州和个别原告缺乏诉讼资格。正如法院所解释的，“过去仅在与预测未来相关的范围内才具有意义”，而原告未能证明政府施压将导致平台再次审查他们的足够具体的可能性。在Ridge Corp.案中，法院以该论述作为框架驳回了前瞻性救济请求。

本争议的第一次上诉于2024年8月终结，当时法院撤销了原始临时禁令，因为Ridge作为不持有全部实质性权利的独占许可人，不属于第35 USC第281条规定的有权起诉的“专利权人”。原告通过增加专利权人Cold Chain, LLC作为共同原告来弥补了这一缺陷，地区法院重新发布了涵盖相同行为的禁令。此次撤销则涉及临时禁令标准本身。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

The Federal Circuit has now rejected preliminary injunctive relief twice in the same roll-up door patent fight. In Ridge Corp. v. Kirk NationaLease Co., No. 25-1254 (Fed. Cir. July 13, 2026), Judge Mayer, writing for a unanimous panel, reversed an injunction barring the defendants from making and selling their competing single-panel insulated truck door. The court found substantial questions of non-infringement under three separate claim limitations and called the irreparable harm showing "speculative at best."

A preliminary injunction is forward-looking relief, and two of the theories supporting this injunction (the case's two non-infringement claims: false patent marking and state law tortious interference) rested entirely on completed past conduct: an advertisement describing the accused door as "patented" while the application was still pending, and a single letter from Kirk's patent counsel warning Whiting Door Manufacturing that it could face royalty liability for building single-panel doors with Ridge. Because the plaintiffs offered no evidence that either act was likely to recur, neither could support an injunction directed at future actions.

The panel looked to the recent First Amendment case of Murthy v. Missouri, 603 U.S. 43 (2024), for support. That is the social media jawboning case, in which the Biden administration was pushing social media companies to remove or downgrade misinformation about COVID-19 and election tampering. The Supreme Court did not decide whether the government’s conduct was unconstitutional. Instead, it held 6-3 that the state and individual plaintiffs lacked standing. As the Court explained, “the past is relevant only insofar as it predicts the future,” and the plaintiffs had not shown a sufficiently concrete likelihood that government pressure would cause the platforms to censor them again. In Ridge Corp., the court used the quote as the framework for denying prospective relief.

The first appeal in this dispute ended in August 2024, when the court vacated the original preliminary injunction because Ridge, an exclusive licensee that did not hold all substantial rights, was not a "patentee" entitled to sue under 35 U.S.C. § 281. The plaintiffs cured the defect by adding patent owner Cold Chain, LLC as co-plaintiff, and the district court reissued an injunction covering the same conduct. This time the reversal reaches the preliminary injunction standard itself.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.
